interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — freenet AG (FNTN.DE)

*Interrogator: Claude Opus 4.8 · Sector: Communication Services · Signal: catalyst · Transcripts: Q1-2024 → Q4/FY-2025 prelim (2026-02-26), live-verified through Q1-2026*

---

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

freenet enters the window (Q1-2024) as a "boring is good" cash machine: a German MVNO with stable mobile, a declining-but-cash-generative Media Broadcast / freenet TV terrestrial legacy, and one growth engine — **waipu.tv** (IPTV/OTT). CEO Christoph Vilanek's framing in Q1-2024 is almost defensive about excitement: *"everything on plan and a bit boring… I'm happy that it's true."* The core story has three legs the whole way through: (i) mobile postpaid net-adds + ARPU discipline, (ii) waipu.tv subscriber growth toward a 2028 ambition, (iii) shareholder returns (80% FCF payout dividend + buyback).

The arc's most important **shift is from "growth at the IPTV unit" to "profitability at the IPTV unit."** Through 2024 the pitch is land-grab: record net-adds, EUR100m Bundesliga + EUR50m Euros content spend, *"around 2 million by the end of the year"* (Q1-2024). By FY-2024 (2025-03-05) Vilanek concedes he *"missed my personal ambition of 2 million somewhat"* (1.940m) and explicitly **re-weights the goal**: *"in 2025 we will focus not only on pure growth, but also on EBITDA and free cash flow contribution… there were still doubts also from your side that waipu… cannot contribute EBITDA."* By FY-2025 prelim, that pivot is declared won: *"around EUR36 million adjusted EBITDA"* at waipu — growth thesis quietly traded for a margin thesis.

A **management regime change** runs underneath: Vilanek (16 years, *"my 63rd analyst quarterly call"*) hands to **Robin John Harries** mid-2025. Harries imports a markedly different vocabulary — *"AI-first telco,"* *"return on ad spend,"* *"conversion rates,"* *"churn reduction… more than 50 initiatives,"* *"customer value management."* The story expands from "operate the cash flows" to "extract efficiency + consolidate the channel."

Two **new legs were bolted on in H2-2025**: (a) the **mobilezone acquisition** (EUR230m, announced Oct-2025, a competitor/"sales machine" doing >1m contracts/yr), and (b) a **1&1 distribution partnership**. The arc thus broadens from organic operator to **channel consolidator**.

The **discordant note that emerges late** and dominates the most recent call is the **MNO (network-operator) contract problem** — first surfaced explicitly at FY-2025 prelim (2026-02-26): a 2024-signed agreement that *"might lead to a minus EUR13 million impact in '25 and to up to EUR50 million negative EBITDA impact for the year '26 to '28."* This is the single most consequential new fact in the entire transcript set and it is a **headwind, not a catalyst** — introduced defensively, mid-negotiation, with the downside already booked into guidance.

---

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Management claim (quarter) | The financials say | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| *"Record year 2024… record EBITDA >EUR520m"* (FY-24) | NI 2024 247M (vs 155M '23); EPS 2.08. Genuine step-up — **but** management itself flags EBITDA was *"stable… without the IP addresses"* — a **one-off IP-address sale** carried the headline. | **Partly true, partly dressed.** Self-disclosed one-off. Honest that they disclosed it; promotional in headlining "record." |
| "Revenues growing / both segments growing" (FY-24, FY-25) | Screener: **revenue −1.5% YoY, −1.5% 3-yr CAGR**; multi-year 2.56→2.56→2.63→2.48→2.44B = **flat-to-declining**. | **Diverges.** Group revenue is *not* growing; mix-shift (high-margin service up, hardware/Gravis/freenet-TV down) flatters gross margin while the top line erodes. Gross margin 25.9% "stable" is accurate; the growth verb is not. |
| "waipu will contribute EBITDA — proven" (FY-25 prelim: ~EUR36m) | Net income trajectory supports a real earnings inflection: NI 198→79→155→247→**271M**; EPS 0.67→2.3. | **Corroborated.** The profitability pivot shows up in the bottom line. This is the most credible part of the story. |
| "80% FCF dividend + up-to-EUR100m buyback" (FY-24/FY-25) | TTM FCF **344M**, P/FCF 7.5x, FCF margin 14.9%. Dividend raised to EUR2.07. **But buyback delivered only ~EUR59–60m of the "up to EUR100m"** and management said no more expected (Q3-25). | **Mostly delivered, with a soft-pedal.** The "up to EUR100m" was anchored on EUR60m (Arnold, Q1-25: *"first EUR60m is easy to calculate… 20% of FCF"*); the EUR100m headline was always aspirational. Yield is real; buyback was under-executed vs the splashier number. |
| "EPS compounding" (implied by guidance) | EPS 5-yr CAGR **+9.2%**; FY-25 EPS 2.3 (+10.6% YoY). **BUT latest-Q EPS YoY −17%** and **TTM diluted EPS 2.21 < FY 2.3**. | **Cracking at the margin.** The annual trend is fine; the *most recent quarter* is already negative YoY — consistent with the MNO drag landing. |
| "Net debt manageable, ~0.5x" (Q3-25) | Scan net debt 959M; **live correction: net debt 596M** (still net debt, not net cash) at 2026-03-31. | **Accurate-ish, improving.** Leverage is modest; the mobilezone EUR230m was bridge-financed, partly offset by the pending CECONOMY ~EUR150m. **Note: input header mislabels 596M as "NEGATIVE = net cash" — it is positive net debt.** |

**Synthesis:** the narrative is *richer on growth* than the top line justifies (group revenue is flat-to-down), and *honest-to-conservative on profitability* (the waipu EBITDA pivot and the cash generation are real and visible in NI/FCF). The story's quality rests on **cash conversion and capital returns**, not on growth.

---

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Trajectory: STABLE, with a late defensive tilt under new management.**

- **Q1-2024 (Vilanek):** Relaxed, offensive on waipu growth. *"No doubt about the guidance. We fully confirmed."* Confidence on net-adds (*"around plus 100,000"* mobile; waipu *"around 2 million by year-end"*). Tone: high, almost casual.
- **FY-2024 (2025-03-05):** Celebratory ("record year") but with a tell — concedes the 2m miss and **re-frames the goalpost to EBITDA**. Defensive language enters on Telefónica O2 churn (*"agreed… not to disclose"*). Outgoing-CEO valedictory tone.
- **Q1-2025 (2025-05-22):** **First clearly defensive quarter.** *"We have, therefore, consequently taken out money of the market… we did on purpose, take down the numbers a little bit."* Reframing a soft net-add quarter as *deliberate* discipline. Heavy pivot to **brand/sponsorship** spend justification ("smart money") — classic offense-to-defense rotation when volume disappoints.
- **Q3-2025 (Harries):** Re-energized *rhetorically* — *"AI-first telco,"* *"I'm truly enjoying this… just getting started"* — but the substance is **efficiency/cost** (churn, conversion, marketing-mix ROAS), i.e., defensive levers dressed in offensive enthusiasm. Confirms guidance.
- **FY-2025 prelim (2026-02-26):** **Most defensive call in the set.** Opens by leading with the bad news: *"we are not happy about [the] agreement with the network provider."* Repeated, visibly uncomfortable non-answers to Joshua Mills (BNP) on the legal basis for the MNO claim. Pricing-discipline slide ("we even increased our prices") is a defensive proof-point.

**Specificity over time:** management became **less specific on the highest-stakes item** (the MNO contract — refusing to name the operator, the legal levers, or contract-end dates) while becoming *more* specific on controllable operational KPIs (net-adds, waipu EBITDA). That asymmetry is the tell.

---

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: MEDIUM (operationally reliable; the screener's beat-rate flags an expectations-setting problem).**

- **Delivered:** FY-2024 guidance confirmed and met; FY-2025 guidance confirmed every quarter and hit (postpaid net-adds *over-delivered* — 306k organic + 240k mobilezone vs "moderate"). waipu EBITDA contribution promised (FY-24) and delivered (~EUR36m, FY-25). Dividend raised as promised.
- **Missed / walked back:** the **2m waipu target** (Vilanek's "personal ambition") missed in 2024; the **EUR100m buyback** delivered at ~EUR60m. The **2028 EUR600m EBITDA ambition** is repeatedly "confirmed" but with growing hedging — Arnold (Q3-25): *"earlier or later… we have to recalculate the levels"*; (FY-25 prelim) the −EUR50m MNO hit lands directly against that bridge.
- **The damning datum:** screener shows **EPS beat 1 of 7 quarters (14%)**. Management *confirms guidance* reliably but **misses consensus EPS** routinely — i.e., they set the Street's bar above where they deliver, or one-offs (IP-address sale, tax) distort the line. This is a **low-quality beat profile** despite "reliable" guidance language.
- **Sandbagging?** No clear sandbag pattern; if anything the reverse — aspirational headline numbers (2m subs, EUR100m buyback) that get trimmed to reality. Management negotiates *down* expectations in soft quarters ("we did on purpose take down the numbers") which is honest but signals they manage the narrative actively.

**Score: Medium.** Trust the FCF/dividend; discount the long-dated ambitions and the splashier round numbers.

---

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

- **waipu net-adds quality vs the O2/Telefónica drag** — pushed every quarter (Stippig, UBS, Dhruva Shah). Management **persistently refuses to disclose the Telefónica churn/acquisition numbers** (*"agreed… not to disclose"*), forcing analysts to back into "~50k/quarter" estimates. A recurring information-withholding pattern around the single biggest swing factor in the growth unit.
- **Buyback size** — Stippig pressed three consecutive calls on whether EUR100m or EUR60m. Answer drifted to "EUR60m done, probably no more." The **bigger number never materialized** — a topic analysts pushed on that quietly turned into a soft miss.
- **2028 EUR600m EBITDA bridge** — Mills, Siyi He, Stippig all probed the step-up required in '27/'28. Management leans on "stable market share × growing IPTV market" — an extrapolation, not a bottom-up build. **Analysts are visibly skeptical the back-end-loaded acceleration is real.**
- **THE MNO CONTRACT (the live one):** Joshua Mills (BNP, FY-25 prelim) delivered the sharpest interrogation of the entire set — *"what levers and what legal backing… do you have to demand access to those tariffs?"* and *"can you confirm that beyond 2028 you'll roll on to a new contract?"* Management (Arnold) **stonewalled**: *"I do not want to talk about [legal actions]… I have not said that it ends in '28."* When the CFO won't confirm contract-end dates or the legal basis for a claim already booked as a EUR50m headwind, **that is the analysts finding the soft spot management can't defend.**
- **Housing-association / Vonovia distribution** — raised repeatedly in 2024; management eventually conceded it *"definitely doesn't work out"* — a growth avenue that died quietly after being floated.

---

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🔴 **MNO/Telefónica contract: a booked EUR50m (2026–28) EBITDA headwind** management can't yet resolve and won't fully explain — the dominant near-term overhang, with the legal basis for any upside left deliberately vague (FY-25 prelim).
🔴 **Group revenue is flat-to-declining** (−1.5% YoY) yet consistently narrated as "growing" via mix-shift — the top-line truth is mature/eroding.
🔴 **EPS beat rate 14% (1/7)** and **latest-Q EPS −17% YoY** — low-quality delivery vs the Street; the most recent quarter is already negative.
🔴 **"Record EBITDA" leaned on a one-off IP-address sale** (self-disclosed, FY-24) — headline quality flattered.
🔴 **Buyback under-delivered** (~EUR60m of "up to EUR100m") — capital-return promise softer than advertised.
🔴 **2028 ambition increasingly hedged** while a fresh EUR50m headwind lands against it.

🟢 **waipu.tv profitability inflection is real and proven** (~EUR36m adj. EBITDA FY-25) — the growth-to-profit pivot delivered, corroborated by NI 247→271M.
🟢 **Genuine, superior FCF** — TTM FCF 344M, FCF margin 14.9%, P/FCF 7.5x; ROIC 11.0%, ROE 12.5% — above most European telco peers.
🟢 **Pricing discipline returning** (FY-25 prelim: front-book prices *raised* through Cyber Week, *"quality over quantity"*) — margin-protective, counter-cyclical to the German price war.
🟢 **mobilezone consolidation closed** (Dec-2025) + 1&1 partnership = incremental, not substitutive, sales and channel power.
🟢 **Modest leverage** (net debt ~596M, ~0.5x EBITDA) with a pending CECONOMY ~EUR150m inflow to delever — balance sheet is not a risk.
🟢 **Record dividend EUR2.07** maintained — the yield thesis is intact.

---

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

- **The CEO handoff is a vocabulary regime change.** Vilanek spoke the language of a *cash-flow operator* (FCF/FTE ratios, "boring is good," sponsorship as cheap brand-building). Harries speaks the language of a *performance-marketing/efficiency operator* ("ROAS," "conversion rates," "churn cohorts," "AI-first," "50 initiatives"). The subtle shift: the *organic* growth story is being replaced by an *operational-leverage + consolidation* story. Read positively, freenet is admitting the German mobile market is ex-growth and is pivoting to take share/cost rather than ride a tide.
- **"AI-first telco" is the new wrapper, but every concrete AI example is a cost/retention lever** (voice bot in service, call-center agent assist, smart pricing) — not a revenue engine. The framing is offensive; the mechanism is defensive margin protection. Don't underwrite AI as a growth catalyst here.
- **Disclosure tightened exactly where it matters.** Across the set, management *volunteers* granular operational color (net-add sources, ad-impression counts, waipu ARPU mechanics) but *withholds* on the two highest-leverage items — Telefónica churn and the MNO contract legal/term details. Selective transparency is itself a signal: they disclose what they control and obscure what they don't.
- **CECONOMY stake framing flipped.** In FY-24 Vilanek called it *"still a strategic investment… we would be the last seller."* By FY-25/Q1-26 it is being *monetized* into the JD.com tender (~EUR150m expected H1-26). A "permanent strategic holding" became a funding source for mobilezone — pragmatic, but a tell that "strategic" labels are flexible when cash is needed.
- **"We took money out of the market on purpose"** (Q1-25) is the kind of phrase that reframes a demand miss as strategy. True in part (ARPU discipline) — but it is also the vocabulary of a management team getting ahead of a soft quarter.

---

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Classification: STABLE, tilting to PIVOTING.** This is a mature, cash-generative, well-managed MVNO+IPTV cash compounder that is *pivoting* its identity from organic operator to **efficiency-driven channel consolidator** under a new CEO, against a flat top line and a fresh, self-inflicted-via-supplier EUR50m EBITDA headwind. The growth narrative (waipu) has already done its best work and is being recast as a *profitability* story — which it has, creditably, delivered. The defensible thesis is **FCF + dividend at 7.5x P/FCF**, not catalyst-driven re-rating.

**The single most important thing the director must weigh before sizing:** the **MNO/Telefónica contract is a two-sided, dated, binding event with the *downside already in the numbers and the upside deliberately unquantified.*** Guidance (adj. EBITDA ~EUR500–530m) already absorbs the −EUR50m hit; a *successful* renegotiation would, by management's own words, "necessitate a stock market announcement due to a change in guidance" — i.e., a genuine positive forcing function. But management cannot/will not articulate the legal leverage behind it, and the base case is the headwind, not the relief. **Do not size this as an asymmetric special-situation; size it as a cheap, high-FCF defensive compounder with one binary contract overhang and a *third-party-dependent* (JD.com) cash inflow.** The two "catalysts" most likely to move it — the CECONOMY proceeds and the Telefónica deal — are **both outside freenet's unilateral control.**

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE

bull_thesis: freenet is a stable, cash-generative German MVNO + IPTV operator trading at 7.8x P/FCF with an ~8% dividend yield (EUR2.07 paid May-2026 on a ~EUR24.7-26 price), priced as if in terminal decline when the actual trajectory is flat revenue with RISING earnings power: NI 155M->247M->271M and EPS 1.30->2.08->2.30 across FY23-25 on disciplined cost-out, ROIC 11% > cost of capital, net debt only ~596M (~1.1x EBITDA). The forensic record is high-quality (credibility 4/5): low-spin management that delivered its single biggest multi-year promise -- waipu.tv flipping from 'can it ever contribute EBITDA?' (2024) to ~EUR36m adjusted EBITDA in FY25 -- and honestly flagged its own one-offs (IP-address sale) and headwinds. Three legs support upside: (1) a SUM-OF-PARTS that exceeds the whole -- a mature Mobile cash engine (waipu hidden inside it now profitable and compounding ~15%, plus a shrinking but ~EUR100m-EBITDA Media Broadcast cash annuity) -- worth a higher blended multiple than the consolidated 7.8x; (2) a ~EUR150m special-situation inflow from the irrevocably-committed CECONOMY (6.7%) stake tendered into JD.com's EUR4.60/share takeover, ~EUR1.1-1.2/share of largely-unrecognized value; (3) emerging pricing power ('we even increased our prices during Cyber Week... this works', FY25) plus mobilezone consolidation (Q1-26 revenue +26% YoY, removing a price-aggressive German rival). You are paid a high single-digit yield to wait while MoS converges. Conditions: the ~EUR50m MNO drag must not worsen, mobilezone synergies must materialize, and the CECONOMY proceeds must eventually clear Austrian review.
bear_thesis: This is a no-growth top line dressed as a compounder: revenue 2.56B->2.44B FY21-25 (-1.5% YoY, -1.5% 3Y CAGR), latest-quarter EPS -17% YoY, and a 14% quarterly beat rate -- the market's ~2% margin-of-safety and 8% yield are a correct discount for structural stagnation, not a mispricing. The equity story rests entirely on cost-out, mix-shift and capital return, all of which are maturing. Three real risks: (1) the SELF-INFLICTED MNO/Telefonica contract -- a 2024-signed deal carrying up to EUR-50m cumulative EBITDA drag FY26-28, already hitting (Q1-26 mobile adj. EBITDA EUR103.9m->EUR93.7m); renegotiation is HOPEFUL not contracted, and management deflected hard on its legal leverage ('I do not want to talk about it'), implying a weak hand -- underwrite the drag as largely sticking. (2) The CECONOMY ~EUR150m inflow is hostage to third-party Austrian regulatory scrutiny of the JD.com deal, now DELAYED past H1-2026 and requiring refiling -- timing is out of management's hands and the proceeds roughly only OFFSET the MNO drag, so it is not a re-rating event. (3) Aspirational-target slippage is chronic: the 2m-waipu target missed (landed 1.94m), the EUR100m buyback was trimmed to ~EUR60m with no further repurchases expected, and the 2028 EUR600m-EBITDA framework is being quietly 'recalculated' while still anchoring the bull case. Under a new CEO leaning on 'AI-first telco' branding and lighter quantification, the risk is paying for a label on an old efficiency story while German consumer weakness, price competition, and the MNO clause cap earnings. Confirming evidence: MNO talks fail / drag widens, mobilezone synergies stay vague, waipu net adds keep being bought with low-ARPU start-up packages.
consensus_delta: Deep-value branch: is the street pricing terminal decline into a company with stable/growing earnings power? Partially. The market reads flat revenue (-1.5%), a 14% beat rate and latest-Q EPS -17% as structural stagnation and assigns a trough 7.8x P/FCF / ~8% yield. What the street under-weights: (1) earnings power is RISING despite flat revenue (NI 155M->271M, EPS 1.30->2.30 FY23-25) on genuine cost discipline + the waipu.tv EBITDA inflection (~EUR36m FY25 from ~zero) -- this is a cash machine getting more efficient, not melting; (2) the ~EUR150m CECONOMY stake is largely unrecognized in the multiple and is a near-realizable asset (tendered at EUR4.60 into JD.com); (3) emerging pricing power + mobilezone consolidation reduce the German price-war risk the bears extrapolate. BUT the street is NOT simply wrong: the MNO/Telefonica clause (-EUR50m cumulative EBITDA) is a real, management-confirmed cap on the mobile engine, and the CECONOMY proceeds roughly only offset it. So the mispricing is modest -- a ~7.8x->9-10x peer re-rate plus carry -- not a violent gap. The incorrect street assumption to fade is 'freenet is a declining MVNO with no levers'; the correct street caution to respect is 'the MNO contract caps the cash engine and the proceeds are timing-uncertain.'
forcing_function: No single hard binary forces a re-rate -- consistent with SOFT_EXTENDED status. The realistic re-rating drivers, in order of asymmetry: (1) RESOLUTION of the JD.com/Ceconomy Austrian review and receipt of the ~EUR150m CECONOMY proceeds (originally guided H1-2026, now delayed/refiled) -- a dated-ish but third-party-controlled inflow that de-levers and crystallizes SoP value; (2) a CONCLUDED MNO/Telefonica renegotiation -- management said a better deal 'would require a stock-market announcement due to a change in guidance', so a signed outcome is the single cleanest catalyst, but it is non-binding and serially-extended; (3) the 2028 guidance refresh ('we will recalculate during 2026') -- if mobilezone synergies + pricing power lift the EUR600m-EBITDA anchor, that re-rates the multiple; (4) FY26 full-year results confirming waipu EBITDA continues compounding ~15% and pricing power holds. The forcing functions are diffuse and slow-burn (resolution-driver independence is decent: CECONOMY-regulatory, MNO-negotiation, and waipu-operational are independent), but none is a primed pain-trade -- hence watchlist.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 month) risks are real but non-fatal -- this is a cash-positive, low-leverage name with no cash-burn hump. (1) MNO drag bites NOW: Q1-26 mobile adj. EBITDA already fell EUR103.9m->EUR93.7m; FY26 group EBITDA is guided to a 'moderate decrease' with up to -EUR50m cumulative MNO impact FY26-28 -- expect soft EBITDA prints that pressure the stock before any re-rate. (2) CECONOMY proceeds SLIP: the JD.com/Ceconomy deal is delayed past H1-2026 and refiled under Austrian investment-control review -- the ~EUR150m inflow management guided for 'first half of the year' may not land on schedule, removing a near-term de-levering/return prop and disappointing anyone underwriting the timing. (3) Debt refi: a bridge loan (12+6+6mo) funds mobilezone plus promissory notes due November -- Arnold flagged a Q1 promissory-note refinancing; in a higher-for-longer regime (Fed on hold, no cut priced until ~Dec) the refi clears at a higher coupon, a small but real interest drag. (4) German consumer weakness + price competition continue to suppress mobile net-add quality. None threatens solvency (Altman-Z 2.3, net debt ~1.1x EBITDA, ~8% covered dividend), but together they cap upside and can produce a better entry on a weak EBITDA print or a CECONOMY-delay headline.
positioning_washout: Limited forced-selling dynamic -- and this is a reason for the 'B/watchlist' rather than 'A'. The shareholder base is yield/value-oriented (8% dividend, low-vol 'boring' telco), and the dividend was just RAISED (EUR2.07) and PAID, so there is no dividend-cut trigger to force mechanical liquidation by income holders -- the base is sticky, not primed to capitulate. The stock already sits near its 52-week low (proximity 0.14, -7% vs SMA200) on weak smart-money score (11/100) and neutral institutional flow (50/100), so weak hands are partly out already, but there is no identifiable forced-seller event (no index deletion, no covenant breach, no pivot that ejects the holder base). The cleaner washout to WAIT FOR is event-driven, not positioning-driven: a soft FY26 EBITDA guide reflecting the full MNO drag, or a CECONOMY/Austria delay headline, either of which could flush the stock toward the EUR22-24 EPV floor and create a fatter entry into the carry.
moderator_conclusion: freenet is a high-quality deep-value/income name with a forensic credibility of 4/5 and a peer-confirmed 'cheap_vs_peers' read, but it is NOT a catalyst trade and the current regime argues against paying up for it today. Applying CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME (2026-06): the regime rewards hard-dated structural special-sits in fat thin-coverage lanes (distressed/deleveraging > spins > forced-sellers) and PENALIZES theses that hinge on a single timing-uncertain, third-party-controlled event or a 'rates-rescue' -- freenet's one positive event (CECONOMY proceeds) is exactly the penalized archetype: it is hostage to Austrian regulators, delayed past H1-2026, and only offsets the self-inflicted MNO EBITDA drag rather than re-rating the equity. The dividend already fired (paid May-2026) and the buyback is spent, so the event re-rate is largely behind us. Reconciling the SoP: base-case fair value ~EUR27 (range EUR24-31) vs a ~EUR24.7-26 price = only +5-9% price upside plus ~8% carry; risk/reward ~+2:1 including the dividend, with a well-protected EUR22-24 EPV/yield floor. That is a real but unspectacular edge with no forcing function and no positioning washout to exploit. VERDICT B (watchlist for capitulation), conviction 3: own the value, but wait for a fatter entry -- specifically a soft FY26 EBITDA guide that prices in the full MNO drag, or a CECONOMY/Austria-delay headline, to buy nearer the EUR22-24 floor and lock in a higher yield + wider MoS. Separately, value_conviction 4: stripping out catalyst_status and the regime tilt, the VALUE case is strong on its own -- 7.8x P/FCF, ~8% covered yield, rising EPS on flat revenue, ROIC 11%, a credible SoP above price, low leverage, and a clean forensic record. The two scores diverge precisely because the asset is cheap and well-run (value 4) while the catalyst/timing setup is soft and partly spent in a regime that does not reward it (conviction 3).

interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — freenet AG (FNTN.DE)

*Interrogator — Claude Opus 4.8. Source: eight consecutive freenet AG earnings-call transcripts (Q1-2024 through preliminary FY-2025 on 2026-02-26) cross-referenced against the screener financial trajectory (FY2021–FY2025 + TTM to 2026-03-31).*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

freenet's story over these eight quarters is the slow handover of a "boring-by-design" cash-cow telco from a 16-year founder-CEO (Christoph Vilanek) to a younger, "AI-first," growth-and-efficiency successor (Robin Harries), with one persistent two-engine thesis underneath: **a mature, cash-generative German MVNO (mobile reselling) funding the scale-up of waipu.tv (IPTV)**.

- **Early (Q1-2024, 2024-05-17):** Vilanek's framing is explicitly *"everything on plan and a bit boring… I'm happy that it's true."* The arc is (a) mobile is maturing/stabilizing (*"more and more stability in the market… our growth slows down"*), (b) waipu is the growth engine being *deliberately* invested into (record net adds of ~139k, EUR100m Bundesliga + EUR50m Euro content spend), and (c) housekeeping — the **Gravis** Apple-retail business is being wound down (*"we have decided to end the Gravis business"*) because two loss-making shops merged *"would not create enough synergies."* Capital return (80% FCF payout dividend) is the spine.

- **Middle (FY-2024 results, 2025-03-05; Q1-2025, 2025-05-22):** Vilanek's victory-lap quarters — *"record year 2024,"* EBITDA EUR521.5m (flattered by a **one-off IP-address sale**, which he candidly flags), dividend up to EUR1.97, a **EUR100m buyback announced**. The thesis *expands* to: waipu must now prove **EBITDA contribution**, not just growth (*"there were still doubts also from your side… waipu is a good product but cannot contribute EBITDA"*). Mobile narrative turns subtly defensive — Q1-2025 admits they *"deliberately took money out of the market,"* i.e. chose not to chase low-margin net adds amid German consumption weakness.

- **Late (Q3-2025, 2025-11-08; prelim FY-2025, 2026-02-26):** Harries takes over and rebrands the company as an *"AI-first telco."* Two genuinely new, *material* developments enter the arc that were absent at the start: (1) the **mobilezone acquisition** (a ~EUR230m M&A pivot — buying a competitor "sales machine" closing >1m contracts/yr — a sharp departure from organic-only history), and (2) the **MNO contract problem**: a 2024-signed network-operator agreement now disclosed to carry a **EUR-13m FY25 / up to EUR-50m cumulative FY26–28 negative EBITDA hit**, with management *"not happy"* and negotiating. The CECONOMY stake (6.7%, ~EUR150m) is now flagged as a pending inflow tied to the JD.com takeover.

**Net arc:** the story *contracted* on quality (a self-inflicted bad MNO contract; a wind-down) even as it *expanded* on ambition (mobilezone M&A; AI; waipu EBITDA inflection). The dividend/FCF spine held throughout. This is a value/income compounder gaining an M&A leg and a special-situation overlay (CECONOMY), not a growth story.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

The actual trajectory (screener): revenue **2.56B → 2.56B → 2.63B → 2.48B → 2.44B** (FY21→25, flat-to-declining, −1.5% YoY); net income **198 → 79 → 155 → 247 → 271M**; EPS **1.62 → 0.67 → 1.30 → 2.08 → 2.30**; TTM FCF 344M; TTM EPS 2.21; ROE 12.5%, ROIC 11.0%; net debt **596M** (live-corrected). Earnings beat rate **1/7 (14%)**.

| Management claim | Quarter | Financial reality | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| "Record year 2024… record EBITDA >520m" | FY24 (3/5/25) | NI jumped 155→247M; EPS 1.30→2.08 (+60%). But Arnold *himself* concedes EBITDA was "stable only" ex the **one-off IP-address sale**. | **Honest-but-flattered.** The record needed a non-recurring item; management disclosed it rather than hid it. Credibility-positive. |
| waipu will "contribute significantly to EBITDA" | FY24→FY25 | Delivered: FY25 waipu adj. EBITDA **~EUR36m** (vs near-zero/doubt in 2024). | **Delivered.** The single most important promise of the period was kept. 🟢 |
| Mobile ARPUs/revenues "stable" | throughout | Group revenue actually *declined* −1.5%; mobile service revenue grew low-single-digit but hardware (Gravis) fell. | **Roughly true at the service-revenue line**, masked by hardware roll-off. Net: stable-to-soft. |
| "No doubt about the guidance… fully confirmed" (EBITDA) | Q1-24, Q3-25 | They confirmed FY25 guidance repeatedly and hit it; FCF grew +5.7% (FY24), +2.8% (9M-25). | **Reliable on the metric they actually guide (EBITDA/FCF).** |
| "2 million waipu by end-2024" (Vilanek's personal ambition) | Q1-24 | Missed — landed at **1.94m** ("I missed my personal ambition of 2 million somewhat"). | **Missed, but owned.** Pattern: aspirational top-line targets slip; the financial guidance (EBITDA/FCF) holds. |
| EPS "+10.6% YoY, 3-yr CAGR +50.9%" / "beat 1/7 qtrs" | screener | EPS CAGR is real but off a depressed 2022 base (EPS 0.67). The **14% beat rate** says they rarely surprise *up* on the quarter. | **Earnings power is real and growing; quarterly upside surprises are not their game.** They guide conservatively on EBITDA and hit it; they are *not* a beat-and-raise stock. |
| mobilezone "incremental sales… nice synergies" | Q3-25, FY25 | Drove Q1-26 revenue +26% YoY (confirmed via web). Synergy quantification is still vague ("50 examples… too early"). | **Revenue accretion real; synergy claims unproven — watch.** |
| MNO contract "risks already reflected in our numbers" | FY25 (2/26/26) | New disclosure of EUR-50m cumulative EBITDA drag. Q1-26 (web) confirms mobile adj. EBITDA fell EUR103.9m→EUR93.7m. | **The drag is real and now hitting.** Management is transparent about it but it is a genuine self-inflicted wound. 🔴 |

**Where the story is richer than the numbers:** the "AI-first telco," 50-initiative churn program, and mobilezone-synergy narrative are aspirational and unquantified. **Where the story is poorer (more honest) than the numbers:** management repeatedly *under-claims* — flagging the IP-address one-off, owning the 2m miss, disclosing the MNO drag proactively. This is a low-spin management team. The financials (flat revenue, rising NI/EPS via cost discipline + waipu EBITDA inflection, 7.8x P/FCF, ~8% dividend yield) corroborate a **stable, cash-generative value name**, not a grower.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Trajectory: STABLE (with a late, honest downshift on one issue).**

- **Q1-2024 (Vilanek):** Relaxed, confident, self-deprecating — *"a bit boring… I'm happy that it's true."* Offensive on waipu, matter-of-fact on Gravis closure.
- **FY-2024 (Vilanek):** Peak confidence — *"record year,"* invents bespoke metrics (EUR196k EBITDA/FTE, EUR961k FCF per shop-opening-day) to dramatize success. *"I personally believe in the 2.3 million and… the 3.5 million."*
- **Q1-2025 (Vilanek):** Still confident but *first defensive notes* — *"we decided… not to outspend the market,"* blaming German consumption weakness and elections. Frames weak net adds as a *choice*. Valedictory tone (his last results call).
- **Q3-2025 (Harries):** New-CEO energy — *"AI-first telco… we are just getting started… a lot of fun."* High enthusiasm, lighter on hard numbers, heavy on initiatives.
- **Prelim FY-2025 (Harries):** Confident on operations (record 306k organic postpaid net adds, waipu EUR36m EBITDA, record EUR2.07 dividend) but **explicitly unhappy on the MNO contract** — *"we are not happy about agreement with the network provider."* This is the one place tone genuinely turned cautious/defensive.

**Specificity shift:** Vilanek was more numerically specific (he'd quote exact net-add splits by channel). Harries leans on qualitative "initiatives" and "potential," quantifying less — a mild credibility watch-item, though he does give hard figures where they matter (net adds, waipu EBITDA, dividend).

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: HIGH on EBITDA/FCF/dividend; MEDIUM on aspirational subscriber/segment targets.**

- They **confirmed FY25 guidance every quarter and delivered it** (FCF +2.8% 9M, on track full-year; record dividend EUR2.07). The things they formally *guide* (EBITDA, FCF, dividend), they hit.
- They **missed aspirational ceilings they explicitly label "ambition" not "guidance"** — Vilanek was careful to call the 2025/2028 numbers an *"ambition,"* and the 2m-waipu and 2.3m-waipu targets slipped (1.94m; then guidance reset to ~2.2m). This is disciplined expectation-management, not deception — but it means the *bull-case* segment targets carry execution risk.
- The **14% quarterly beat rate** is consistent: this is a *deliver-the-guidance, don't-sandbag-for-beats* culture. No evidence of serial sandbagging to manufacture beats; rather, conservative annual guides that are met.
- **One credibility ding:** the FY25 outlook had to absorb a EUR-50m cumulative MNO drag that stemmed from a **contract they themselves signed in 2024** and only fully surfaced the magnitude of in early 2026. They disclosed it cleanly, but it reflects a planning/negotiation miss, not just bad luck.

Net: **management says what it will do on cash and does it.** Score reliability **High** on the cash metrics that anchor a value thesis.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

- **waipu monetization vs. volume (recurring, every call):** analysts (Stippig/Warburg, UBS, Citi, BNP) repeatedly probe whether growth is being bought with low-ARPU "start-up packages" and bundles, and whether the 2028 EBITDA step-up (to the implied EUR600m group target) is credible. Management answer is consistently "product is superior, we'll monetize via upselling + CTV advertising" — **plausible but not yet proven at scale**; advertising is still only ~16–30% of usage monetized.
- **The MNO contract (FY25, sharply pressed by Joshua Mills/BNP):** the most uncomfortable exchange in the set. Mills drilled on freenet's *legal leverage* to force better tariffs from the MNO; Arnold **deflected hard** — *"about the legal actions and possibilities, I do not want to talk about… we see possibilities there."* When Mills asked if the contract expires 2028, Arnold corrected: *"I have not said that it ends in '28."* This is the clearest non-answer in the transcripts and sits over a real EUR50m liability. 🔴
- **Buyback follow-through:** analysts pressed repeatedly on whether the EUR100m buyback would be fully executed; answer drifted from "up to EUR100m" to "we did the EUR60m… from today's point of view I would not expect additional buybacks." **Under-delivery vs. the headline EUR100m** — a soft spot for an income/return thesis.
- **CECONOMY stake & change-of-control (Stippig, FY24):** management called it *"still a strategic investment… we would be the last seller"* — then within a year committed it to the JD.com takeover. Not a contradiction (they were tendered into a control transaction, exactly the carve-out they named), but worth noting the stance flipped from "last seller" to "committed seller."
- **2028 long-term guidance review:** analysts want an updated 2028 framework given mobilezone + MNO drag; management keeps deferring (*"we will recalculate during 2026"*). The unrefreshed EUR600m EBITDA / EUR3m-waipu 2028 anchor is doing a lot of work in the bull case while being under active revision.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🟢 **waipu EBITDA inflection delivered** — from "can it ever contribute?" (2024) to **~EUR36m adj. EBITDA FY25**; the central multi-year promise was kept.
🟢 **Capital-return discipline & transparency** — 80%-of-FCF dividend policy honored (record EUR2.07), one-off IP-sale flagged honestly, low-spin management.
🟢 **Cost discipline visible in the numbers** — flat/declining revenue but NI 155→271M and EPS 1.30→2.30 over FY23→25; ROIC 11% > cost of capital; net debt manageable at 596M (~1.1x EBITDA).
🟢 **mobilezone consolidation** — buying a profitable competitor at a sensible price, immediately revenue-accretive (Q1-26 rev +26%), removing a price-aggressive rival from the German market (industry-rationalizing).
🟢 **Pricing power emerging** — FY25: *"we even increased our prices… during Cyber Week… this works"* — a genuine shift from front-book price war to value-over-volume.

🔴 **Self-inflicted MNO contract** — EUR-13m FY25 / up to EUR-50m FY26–28 EBITDA drag from a 2024-signed deal; renegotiation is *hopeful, not contracted*; legal leverage deflected.
🔴 **CECONOMY proceeds delayed** — the ~EUR150m inflow is now hostage to **Austrian regulatory scrutiny of the JD.com deal (delayed past H1-2026; refiling required)** — a third-party, out-of-management's-hands timing risk.
🔴 **Aspirational-target slippage** — 2m-waipu missed; buyback trimmed EUR100m→EUR60m; 2028 framework deferred/under-revision while still anchoring the bull case.
🔴 **Quarterly beat rate 14%** and *negative latest-Q EPS YoY (−17%)* + revenue declining — this is a no-growth top line; the equity story rests entirely on cost-out, mix-shift to waipu, capital return, and the CECONOMY special-sit, not organic expansion.
🔴 **CEO transition + lighter quantification** under Harries — more "initiatives"/"AI" narrative, less numeric specificity than Vilanek.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

- **Terminology shift "guidance" → "ambition":** Vilanek deliberately downgraded forward targets to "ambition," pre-creating room to miss the top line while protecting the EBITDA/FCF guide. A tell that the *segment* targets are softer than they sound.
- **"AI-first telco" (Harries) is new packaging on an old efficiency story** — the substance is churn-reduction and marketing-mix optimization (ROAS discipline), not a tech re-rating. Don't pay a growth multiple for the label.
- **From organic purist to acquirer:** Vilanek explicitly *rejected* cooperation/M&A detours (*"why should I do a cooperation if I can take the shortcut and just acquire the customers"* re housing assocs); Harries pivoted to a EUR230m competitor acquisition (mobilezone) and a 1&1 reseller partnership. A real strategic-culture change worth underwriting.
- **CECONOMY stance flip** — "we would be the last seller" (FY24) → irrevocably committed to JD.com's EUR4.60 offer (2025–26). Management got the exact change-of-control event it had reserved for, and took it. Reads as opportunistic monetization of a non-core, illiquid stake — a positive for SoP clarity.
- **The MNO non-answer is the loudest signal.** A management this transparent elsewhere going terse on legal leverage suggests their negotiating hand is **weaker** than the "optimistic… good mood" framing implies. Underwrite the EUR50m drag as *likely to largely stick*, not be negotiated away.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**Classification: STABLE, with a PIVOTING overlay.** The core engine (cash-generative German MVNO + capital return) is stable-to-quietly-improving (waipu EBITDA inflection, pricing power, cost-out lifting EPS on flat revenue). The pivot is real but contained: an M&A leg (mobilezone), an "AI-first" efficiency rebrand, and a one-time special-situation (the CECONOMY/JD.com stake monetization). It is **not deteriorating** — but it is no longer purely "boring."

**The single most important thing to weigh before sizing:** This is a **value/income + special-situation** name, not a catalyst trade. The thesis is "you are paid ~8% dividend yield at 7.8x P/FCF to own a stable cash compounder while two offsetting events resolve — a **positive ~EUR150m CECONOMY inflow** (delayed, third-party-controlled by Austrian regulators) and a **negative ~EUR50m MNO EBITDA drag** (real, hitting now, only hopefully negotiable)." The director must net these honestly: the CECONOMY proceeds roughly offset two-to-three years of MNO drag, so neither is a true re-rating catalyst — **the return is the carry (dividend + modest MoS convergence), not an event pop.** Size it as a core-value/income position, not an event-driven swing.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE

consensus_delta: Deep-value branch: is the street pricing terminal decline into a company with stable/growing earnings power? Partially. The market reads flat revenue (-1.5%), a 14% beat rate and latest-Q EPS -17% as structural stagnation and assigns a trough 7.8x P/FCF / ~8% yield. What the street under-weights: (1) earnings power is RISING despite flat revenue (NI 155M->271M, EPS 1.30->2.30 FY23-25) on genuine cost discipline + the waipu.tv EBITDA inflection (~EUR36m FY25 from ~zero) -- this is a cash machine getting more efficient, not melting; (2) the ~EUR150m CECONOMY stake is largely unrecognized in the multiple and is a near-realizable asset (tendered at EUR4.60 into JD.com); (3) emerging pricing power + mobilezone consolidation reduce the German price-war risk the bears extrapolate. BUT the street is NOT simply wrong: the MNO/Telefonica clause (-EUR50m cumulative EBITDA) is a real, management-confirmed cap on the mobile engine, and the CECONOMY proceeds roughly only offset it. So the mispricing is modest -- a ~7.8x->9-10x peer re-rate plus carry -- not a violent gap. The incorrect street assumption to fade is 'freenet is a declining MVNO with no levers'; the correct street caution to respect is 'the MNO contract caps the cash engine and the proceeds are timing-uncertain.'
forcing_function: No single hard binary forces a re-rate -- consistent with SOFT_EXTENDED status. The realistic re-rating drivers, in order of asymmetry: (1) RESOLUTION of the JD.com/Ceconomy Austrian review and receipt of the ~EUR150m CECONOMY proceeds (originally guided H1-2026, now delayed/refiled) -- a dated-ish but third-party-controlled inflow that de-levers and crystallizes SoP value; (2) a CONCLUDED MNO/Telefonica renegotiation -- management said a better deal 'would require a stock-market announcement due to a change in guidance', so a signed outcome is the single cleanest catalyst, but it is non-binding and serially-extended; (3) the 2028 guidance refresh ('we will recalculate during 2026') -- if mobilezone synergies + pricing power lift the EUR600m-EBITDA anchor, that re-rates the multiple; (4) FY26 full-year results confirming waipu EBITDA continues compounding ~15% and pricing power holds. The forcing functions are diffuse and slow-burn (resolution-driver independence is decent: CECONOMY-regulatory, MNO-negotiation, and waipu-operational are independent), but none is a primed pain-trade -- hence watchlist.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 month) risks are real but non-fatal -- this is a cash-positive, low-leverage name with no cash-burn hump. (1) MNO drag bites NOW: Q1-26 mobile adj. EBITDA already fell EUR103.9m->EUR93.7m; FY26 group EBITDA is guided to a 'moderate decrease' with up to -EUR50m cumulative MNO impact FY26-28 -- expect soft EBITDA prints that pressure the stock before any re-rate. (4) German consumer weakness + price competition continue to suppress mobile net-add quality. None threatens solvency (Altman-Z 2.3, net debt ~1.1x EBITDA, ~8% covered dividend), but together they cap upside and can produce a better entry on a weak EBITDA print or a CECONOMY-delay headline.
positioning_washout: Limited forced-selling dynamic -- and this is a reason for the 'B/watchlist' rather than 'A'. The shareholder base is yield/value-oriented (8% dividend, low-vol 'boring' telco), and the dividend was just RAISED (EUR2.07) and PAID, so there is no dividend-cut trigger to force mechanical liquidation by income holders -- the base is sticky, not primed to capitulate. The stock already sits near its 52-week low (proximity 0.14, -7% vs SMA200) on weak smart-money score (11/100) and neutral institutional flow (50/100), so weak hands are partly out already, but there is no identifiable forced-seller event (no index deletion, no covenant breach, no pivot that ejects the holder base). The cleaner washout to WAIT FOR is event-driven, not positioning-driven: a soft FY26 EBITDA guide reflecting the full MNO drag, or a CECONOMY/Austria delay headline, either of which could flush the stock toward the EUR22-24 EPV floor and create a fatter entry into the carry.
moderator_conclusion: freenet is a high-quality deep-value/income name with a forensic credibility of 4/5 and a peer-confirmed 'cheap_vs_peers' read, but it is NOT a catalyst trade and the current regime argues against paying up for it today. Applying CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME (2026-06): the regime rewards hard-dated structural special-sits in fat thin-coverage lanes (distressed/deleveraging > spins > forced-sellers) and PENALIZES theses that hinge on a single timing-uncertain, third-party-controlled event or a 'rates-rescue' -- freenet's one positive event (CECONOMY proceeds) is exactly the penalized archetype: it is hostage to Austrian regulators, delayed past H1-2026, and only offsets the self-inflicted MNO EBITDA drag rather than re-rating the equity. The dividend already fired (paid May-2026) and the buyback is spent, so the event re-rate is largely behind us. Reconciling the SoP: base-case fair value ~EUR27 (range EUR24-31) vs a ~EUR24.7-26 price = only +5-9% price upside plus ~8% carry; risk/reward ~+2:1 including the dividend, with a well-protected EUR22-24 EPV/yield floor. interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — freenet AG (FNTN.DE)

*Interrogator — Claude Opus 4.8. Source: eight consecutive freenet AG earnings-call transcripts (Q1-2024 through preliminary FY-2025 on 2026-02-26) cross-referenced against the screener financial trajectory (FY2021–FY2025 + TTM to 2026-03-31).*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

freenet's story over these eight quarters is the slow handover of a "boring-by-design" cash-cow telco from a 16-year founder-CEO (Christoph Vilanek) to a younger, "AI-first," growth-and-efficiency successor (Robin Harries), with one persistent two-engine thesis underneath: **a mature, cash-generative German MVNO (mobile reselling) funding the scale-up of waipu.tv (IPTV)**.

- **Early (Q1-2024, 2024-05-17):** Vilanek's framing is explicitly *"everything on plan and a bit boring… I'm happy that it's true."* The arc is (a) mobile is maturing/stabilizing (*"more and more stability in the market… our growth slows down"*), (b) waipu is the growth engine being *deliberately* invested into (record net adds of ~139k, EUR100m Bundesliga + EUR50m Euro content spend), and (c) housekeeping — the **Gravis** Apple-retail business is being wound down (*"we have decided to end the Gravis business"*) because two loss-making shops merged *"would not create enough synergies."* Capital return (80% FCF payout dividend) is the spine.

- **Middle (FY-2024 results, 2025-03-05; Q1-2025, 2025-05-22):** Vilanek's victory-lap quarters — *"record year 2024,"* EBITDA EUR521.5m (flattered by a **one-off IP-address sale**, which he candidly flags), dividend up to EUR1.97, a **EUR100m buyback announced**. The thesis *expands* to: waipu must now prove **EBITDA contribution**, not just growth (*"there were still doubts also from your side… waipu is a good product but cannot contribute EBITDA"*). Mobile narrative turns subtly defensive — Q1-2025 admits they *"deliberately took money out of the market,"* i.e. chose not to chase low-margin net adds amid German consumption weakness.

- **Late (Q3-2025, 2025-11-08; prelim FY-2025, 2026-02-26):** Harries takes over and rebrands the company as an *"AI-first telco."* Two genuinely new, *material* developments enter the arc that were absent at the start: (1) the **mobilezone acquisition** (a ~EUR230m M&A pivot — buying a competitor "sales machine" closing >1m contracts/yr — a sharp departure from organic-only history), and (2) the **MNO contract problem**: a 2024-signed network-operator agreement now disclosed to carry a **EUR-13m FY25 / up to EUR-50m cumulative FY26–28 negative EBITDA hit**, with management *"not happy"* and negotiating. The CECONOMY stake (6.7%, ~EUR150m) is now flagged as a pending inflow tied to the JD.com takeover.

**Net arc:** the story *contracted* on quality (a self-inflicted bad MNO contract; a wind-down) even as it *expanded* on ambition (mobilezone M&A; AI; waipu EBITDA inflection). The dividend/FCF spine held throughout. This is a value/income compounder gaining an M&A leg and a special-situation overlay (CECONOMY), not a growth story.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

The actual trajectory (screener): revenue **2.56B → 2.56B → 2.63B → 2.48B → 2.44B** (FY21→25, flat-to-declining, −1.5% YoY); net income **198 → 79 → 155 → 247 → 271M**; EPS **1.62 → 0.67 → 1.30 → 2.08 → 2.30**; TTM FCF 344M; TTM EPS 2.21; ROE 12.5%, ROIC 11.0%; net debt **596M** (live-corrected). Earnings beat rate **1/7 (14%)**.

| Management claim | Quarter | Financial reality | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| "Record year 2024… record EBITDA >520m" | FY24 (3/5/25) | NI jumped 155→247M; EPS 1.30→2.08 (+60%). But Arnold *himself* concedes EBITDA was "stable only" ex the **one-off IP-address sale**. | **Honest-but-flattered.** The record needed a non-recurring item; management disclosed it rather than hid it. Credibility-positive. |
| waipu will "contribute significantly to EBITDA" | FY24→FY25 | Delivered: FY25 waipu adj. EBITDA **~EUR36m** (vs near-zero/doubt in 2024). | **Delivered.** The single most important promise of the period was kept. 🟢 |
| Mobile ARPUs/revenues "stable" | throughout | Group revenue actually *declined* −1.5%; mobile service revenue grew low-single-digit but hardware (Gravis) fell. | **Roughly true at the service-revenue line**, masked by hardware roll-off. Net: stable-to-soft. |
| "No doubt about the guidance… fully confirmed" (EBITDA) | Q1-24, Q3-25 | They confirmed FY25 guidance repeatedly and hit it; FCF grew +5.7% (FY24), +2.8% (9M-25). | **Reliable on the metric they actually guide (EBITDA/FCF).** |
| "2 million waipu by end-2024" (Vilanek's personal ambition) | Q1-24 | Missed — landed at **1.94m** ("I missed my personal ambition of 2 million somewhat"). | **Missed, but owned.** Pattern: aspirational top-line targets slip; the financial guidance (EBITDA/FCF) holds. |
| EPS "+10.6% YoY, 3-yr CAGR +50.9%" / "beat 1/7 qtrs" | screener | EPS CAGR is real but off a depressed 2022 base (EPS 0.67). The **14% beat rate** says they rarely surprise *up* on the quarter. | **Earnings power is real and growing; quarterly upside surprises are not their game.** They guide conservatively on EBITDA and hit it; they are *not* a beat-and-raise stock. |
| mobilezone "incremental sales… nice synergies" | Q3-25, FY25 | Drove Q1-26 revenue +26% YoY (confirmed via web). Synergy quantification is still vague ("50 examples… too early"). | **Revenue accretion real; synergy claims unproven — watch.** |
| MNO contract "risks already reflected in our numbers" | FY25 (2/26/26) | New disclosure of EUR-50m cumulative EBITDA drag. Q1-26 (web) confirms mobile adj. EBITDA fell EUR103.9m→EUR93.7m. | **The drag is real and now hitting.** Management is transparent about it but it is a genuine self-inflicted wound. 🔴 |

**Where the story is richer than the numbers:** the "AI-first telco," 50-initiative churn program, and mobilezone-synergy narrative are aspirational and unquantified. **Where the story is poorer (more honest) than the numbers:** management repeatedly *under-claims* — flagging the IP-address one-off, owning the 2m miss, disclosing the MNO drag proactively. This is a low-spin management team. The financials (flat revenue, rising NI/EPS via cost discipline + waipu EBITDA inflection, 7.8x P/FCF, ~8% dividend yield) corroborate a **stable, cash-generative value name**, not a grower.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Trajectory: STABLE (with a late, honest downshift on one issue).**

- **Q1-2024 (Vilanek):** Relaxed, confident, self-deprecating — *"a bit boring… I'm happy that it's true."* Offensive on waipu, matter-of-fact on Gravis closure.
- **FY-2024 (Vilanek):** Peak confidence — *"record year,"* invents bespoke metrics (EUR196k EBITDA/FTE, EUR961k FCF per shop-opening-day) to dramatize success. *"I personally believe in the 2.3 million and… the 3.5 million."*
- **Q1-2025 (Vilanek):** Still confident but *first defensive notes* — *"we decided… not to outspend the market,"* blaming German consumption weakness and elections. Frames weak net adds as a *choice*. Valedictory tone (his last results call).
- **Q3-2025 (Harries):** New-CEO energy — *"AI-first telco… we are just getting started… a lot of fun."* High enthusiasm, lighter on hard numbers, heavy on initiatives.
- **Prelim FY-2025 (Harries):** Confident on operations (record 306k organic postpaid net adds, waipu EUR36m EBITDA, record EUR2.07 dividend) but **explicitly unhappy on the MNO contract** — *"we are not happy about agreement with the network provider."* This is the one place tone genuinely turned cautious/defensive.

**Specificity shift:** Vilanek was more numerically specific (he'd quote exact net-add splits by channel). Harries leans on qualitative "initiatives" and "potential," quantifying less — a mild credibility watch-item, though he does give hard figures where they matter (net adds, waipu EBITDA, dividend).

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Reliability: HIGH on EBITDA/FCF/dividend; MEDIUM on aspirational subscriber/segment targets.**

- They **confirmed FY25 guidance every quarter and delivered it** (FCF +2.8% 9M, on track full-year; record dividend EUR2.07). The things they formally *guide* (EBITDA, FCF, dividend), they hit.

# Forensic Dossier — freenet AG (FNTN.DE)

*Interrogator: Claude Opus 4.8 · Sector: Communication Services · Signal: deep_value (iv15) · Transcripts span 2024-05 (Q1'24) → 2025-11 (Q3'25), four calls, two CEO handovers*

---

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

freenet's core story is remarkably stable across the window — and that stability is itself the most important finding. It is a German MVNO (no owned network; resells DT/Vodafone/Telefonica capacity) plus a growth IPTV asset (waipu.tv) plus a structurally-declining terrestrial-TV/broadcast tail (freenet TV / Media Broadcast). The whole investment debate hinges on the cross-over: **can waipu.tv's EBITDA contribution outgrow the freenet-TV runoff fast enough to keep group EBITDA on its "≥€600m by 2028" glide path while mobile stays roughly flat.**

- **Q1'24 (Vilanek):** "everything on plan and a bit boring… I'm happy that it's true." The arc opens defensively honest: mobile growth "slows down… more and more stability in the market"; waipu is the growth engine (record net adds ~139k); Gravis (Apple retail) is being **shut down** — the first quiet admission that a legacy distribution leg is dead. Capital-allocation frame: 80% of FCF to dividend, remainder to buyback.
- **FY'24 (Mar'25):** Victory-lap tone — "record year," EBITDA €521.5m (flattered by a one-off **IP-address sale**; CFO concedes "without the IP addresses, the EBITDA was coming in on a stable level only"). Dividend raised to €1.97, €100m buyback floated. waipu missed Vilanek's personal 2.0m target (landed 1.94m). First framing of waipu as an **EBITDA contributor**, not just a subscriber-growth story — a deliberate pivot to answer the bear ("waipu is a good product but cannot contribute EBITDA").
- **Q1'25 (May'25):** "sound start." Mobile net adds deliberately throttled ("we took money out of the market") to protect ARPU/margin — an offensive-sounding euphemism for a defensive volume decision. Vilanek's farewell call (16 yrs).
- **Q3'25 (Nov'25, new CEO Robin Harries):** Sharp tonal reset. "transformation into an **AI-first telco**," 50+ churn-reduction initiatives, web-shop conversion optimization, and the two structural moves that now dominate the thesis: the **mobilezone acquisition** (a ~1m-contracts/yr German sales machine; sparhandy/deinhandy brands) and the pending **CECONOMY stake exit** (~€150m in). The story expands from "harvest a mature cash cow" to "consolidate the German reseller channel + monetize the waipu ad-inventory + de-lever via asset sales."

**Drivers quietly dropped:** Gravis (shut); freenet-TV ever finding a "floor" (management has waited "7-8 years" and stopped pretending); the housing-association (Vonovia) distribution channel for waipu (explicitly abandoned — "it definitely doesn't work out"). **Drivers introduced:** CTV/addressable advertising on waipu (ProSiebenSat.1 deal live, ~30% of usage monetizable), Media Broadcast field-service pivot (EV-charger maintenance), mobilezone consolidation.

---

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Mgmt claim | Quarter | Hard financials | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| "record year… record EBITDA >€520m" | FY'24 | NI 2024 €247m (vs €155m '23); EPS €2.08 | TRUE but **flattered** — CFO admits ex-IP-address sale EBITDA was merely *stable*. Story richer than the underlying. |
| Revenue "stable / growing" | all | Rev 2021 2.56B → 2024 2.48B → 2025 2.44B; **3-yr CAGR −1.5%** | DIVERGES. Group revenue is in slow secular decline. "Stable" is generous; mix-shift (high-margin service rev up, hardware/Gravis down) is the real story. |
| waipu "double-digit growth… 2.3m by end-'25, 3.5m by 2028" | Q1'24→Q3'25 | Net adds decelerated 139k (Q1'24) → 60k (Q1'25); 2.0m target *missed* (1.94m) | PARTLY DELIVERS but **serially shaved**. The growth is real but every milestone has been trimmed; "I personally believe in 2.3m" is conviction language, not a commitment. |
| "waipu will contribute significantly to EBITDA" | FY'24→Q3'25 | Group adj. EBITDA +1.6% 9M'25; Q3 +4% | CREDIBLE and improving — the one claim the numbers are starting to corroborate. |
| Mobile "small growth… stable ARPU" | all | Service rev grinding up low-single-digit; postpaid +182k FY'24, +190k 9M'25 | TRUE. Mobile is a low-growth, stable-margin annuity — exactly as described. |

**Net:** the financials are *solid but not exciting* — a flat-to-declining top line, stable-to-rising margins, strong FCF, modest deleveraging.
